 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fo8i","title":"Implement moderation appeal process","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-u8t6.

## Task
Allow users to appeal moderation decisions.

## Why This Matters
- Fairness to users
- Catch false positives
- Build trust
- Legal protection

## Appeal Process

### 1. Submit Appeal
- From suspension/removal notice
- Explain why decision wrong
- Provide evidence
- One appeal per action

### 2. Review Queue
- Admin reviews appeals
- See original violation
- See user explanation
- Make decision

### 3. Outcomes
- Upheld (keep action)
- Overturned (reverse action)
- Modified (reduce severity)

### 4. Communication
- Notify user of outcome
- Explain reasoning
- Final decision (no re-appeal)

## Database
```typescript
export const moderationAppeals = pgTable('moderation_appeals', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  
  // What is being appealed
  moderationActionId: uuid('moderation_action_id').notNull(),
  actionType: text('action_type').notNull(), // content_removed, account_suspended, etc.
  
  // Appellant
  userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  
  // Appeal content
  reason: text('reason').notNull(),
  evidence: text('evidence'),
  
  // Status
  status: text('status').default('pending'), // pending, reviewing, upheld, overturned, modified
  
  // Review
  reviewedBy: uuid('reviewed_by').references(() => userProfiles.id),
  reviewedAt: timestamp('reviewed_at'),
  reviewNotes: text('review_notes'),
  outcome: text('outcome'),
  
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});
```

## UI Components
- AppealForm - Submit appeal
- AppealStatus - Track appeal status
- AppealQueue - Admin queue
- AppealReview - Admin review panel

## API Endpoints
- POST /api/appeals - Submit appeal
- GET /api/appeals/[id] - Get appeal status
- GET /api/admin/appeals - List appeals (admin)
- PATCH /api/admin/appeals/[id] - Resolve appeal (admin)

## Limits
- 1 appeal per moderation action
- 7 days to submit appeal
- Admin has 14 days to review
- No re-appeals after decision

## Files to Create
- src/lib/db/schema/appeals.ts
- src/app/api/appeals/route.ts
- src/app/(app)/appeals/[id]/page.tsx
- src/app/(app)/admin/appeals/page.tsx
- src/components/moderation/appeal-form.tsx

## Success Criteria
- Appeals can be submitted
- Admin can review efficiently
- Users notified of outcomes
- Process is fair and documented

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fuc1","title":"UX Flow Improvements: Filter discoverability and clarity","description":"# Epic: UX Flow Improvements

## Background & Problem Statement

User testing and UX audit revealed several friction points in the main browsing experience:

### Issue 1: "Clear All Filters" Discoverability
**Current state:** Plain text link that only appears when filters are active
**Problem:** 
- Users don't notice it (blends with surrounding text)
- No visual affordance before filters are applied
- Uses indigo accent inconsistent with design system

### Issue 2: Featured Section Confusion
**Current state:** Featured prompts appear before filter controls
**Problem:**
- Filters don't affect featured section (confusing)
- No clear visual boundary between "featured" and "browse" modes
- Users may think filtering is broken

### Issue 3: Basket Empty State
**Current state:** Basic icon + text ("Your basket is empty")
**Problem:**
- Not engaging or helpful
- No CTA to guide users
- Missed opportunity for onboarding

### Issue 4: Active Filter Visibility
**Current state:** Only text description below results header
**Problem:**
- Easy to miss that filters are active
- No visual chips/badges for individual filters
- Can't remove individual tags without going back to filter section

## Success Criteria
- [ ] "Clear filters" is a visible button (ghost variant with X icon)
- [ ] Featured section has clear visual separation
- [ ] Basket empty state includes helpful CTA
- [ ] Active filters shown as removable chips
- [ ] All changes maintain Stripe/Linear aesthetic

## Design Principles
- Make hidden things visible
- Reduce cognitive load
- Provide clear feedback
- Guide users toward actions

## Dependencies
- Should be done AFTER color consistency (Phase 1)
- These are additive features, not breaking changes

## Estimated Scope
- 3-4 files to modify
- Medium complexity (some new components)
- Requires design decisions on specifics","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:37:53.772613318-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T00:37:53.772613318-05:00"}
-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-g0oe","title":"Fix PromptDetailModal.tsx: Replace zinc/indigo with neutral","description":"# Task: Unify colors in PromptDetailModal.tsx

## Context
The prompt detail modal is a high-traffic component (every prompt view goes through it). It currently uses ~25 instances of zinc-* and 5+ instances of indigo-*, creating visual disconnect with the newly refactored cards and filters.

## Specific Changes Required

### Badge and header styling (lines 223-236)
```tsx
// Before
<Badge variant="outline" className="... bg-zinc-50 dark:bg-zinc-800/50">

// After
<Badge variant="outline" className="... bg-neutral-50 dark:bg-neutral-800/50">
```

### Tag styling (lines 243-250)
```tsx
// Before
className="text-[11px] text-zinc-500 dark:text-zinc-500 bg-zinc-100 dark:bg-zinc-800/70 ..."

// After (also fix text-[11px] → text-xs)
className="text-xs text-neutral-500 dark:text-neutral-500 bg-neutral-100 dark:bg-neutral-800/70 ..."
```

### Variables section gradient (lines 256-257)
```tsx
// Before - Overly complex gradient that breaks Stripe aesthetic
bg-gradient-to-b from-indigo-50/50 to-transparent dark:from-indigo-950/20

// After - Clean, subtle neutral styling
bg-neutral-50/50 dark:bg-neutral-900/30 border border-neutral-200/50 dark:border-neutral-800/50
```
Note: This is a design simplification, not just a color swap.

### Form input backgrounds (lines 276, 284)
```tsx
// Before
className="... bg-white dark:bg-zinc-900/50"

// After
className="... bg-white dark:bg-neutral-900/50"
```

### Context textarea (line 308)
```tsx
// Before
className="... bg-zinc-50 dark:bg-zinc-900/50"

// After
className="... bg-neutral-50 dark:bg-neutral-900/50"
```

### Prompt preview section (lines 315-320)
```tsx
// Before
border-zinc-200/80 dark:border-zinc-800/80 bg-zinc-50 dark:bg-zinc-900/50
// Fade gradient:
from-zinc-50 dark:from-zinc-900/50

// After
border-neutral-200/80 dark:border-neutral-800/80 bg-neutral-50 dark:bg-neutral-900/50
from-neutral-50 dark:from-neutral-900/50
```

### Button hover states (lines 386, 394)
```tsx
// Before
hover:bg-zinc-100 dark:hover:bg-zinc-800

// After
hover:bg-neutral-100 dark:hover:bg-neutral-800
```

## Also Fix Typography
- Line 233: `text-[11px]` → `text-xs` (token count)
- Line 246: `text-[11px]` → `text-xs` (tags)
- Line 288: `text-[11px]` → `text-xs` (variable description)

## Design Rationale
- Modal should feel like a natural extension of the card that opened it
- Variables section gradient was too "branded" - simplify for Stripe aesthetic
- All form inputs should have consistent background treatment

## Testing
1. Open modal from PromptCard - verify visual continuity
2. Check all form inputs in dark mode
3. Verify variables section doesn't feel "flat" after simplification
4. Test with prompts that have many variables (grid layout)

## Dependency
- Can be done in parallel with page.tsx changes
- Should maintain consistency with PromptCard.tsx styling","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:35:27.872995897-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T00:35:27.872995897-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-g0oe","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-s6ex","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:36:48.101444409-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ms6","title":"Configure Cloudflare DNS for jeffreysprompts.com","description":"**Details:**
- Set up DNS records in Cloudflare
- Point domain to Vercel
- Configure SSL/TLS
- Enable caching and security features

**DNS Records:**
```
Type  Name    Content                   Proxy
CNAME @       cname.vercel-dns.com      Yes
CNAME www     cname.vercel-dns.com      Yes
```

**SSL/TLS Settings:**
- Mode: Full (strict)
- Always Use HTTPS: On
- Automatic HTTPS Rewrites: On
- HSTS: Enabled with long max-age

**Caching:**
- Browser Cache TTL: 4 hours
- Respect Existing Headers: Yes

**Security:**
- Bot Fight Mode: On
- Security Level: Medium

**Verification:**
- Add Vercel domain verification TXT record if required
- Test SSL certificate issuance

**Reasoning:** Cloudflare provides CDN, SSL, and DDoS protection. Proper DNS setup ensures reliable, secure access to jeffreysprompts.com.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:33:27.131701883-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:14:41.769783804-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:14:41.769783804-05:00","close_reason":"Site is live at jeffreysprompts.com - DNS configured with Cloudflare pointing to Vercel. The featured prompts section sits between Hero (neutral) and the main grid (neutral), but uses old zinc/indigo colors, creating a visual "seam" in the page.

- This matches the Nav.tsx link styling pattern

## Dependency

- Uses same PromptCard components, so cards will already look correct

## Testing

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-n299","title":"Implement device code flow for remote/SSH login","description":"## Task
Implement the device code flow for `jfp login --remote` that works on headless/SSH machines.

## Why
Many power users run the CLI on remote servers via SSH where there's no browser. The device code flow:
1. User runs `jfp login --remote` on server
2. CLI shows a URL and 6-character code
3. User visits URL on ANY device (phone, laptop, etc.)
4. User enters code after authenticating
5. CLI receives token via polling

This is the same pattern used by:
- GitHub CLI (`gh auth login`)
- Azure CLI (`az login`)
- AWS SSO (`aws sso login`)

## Implementation

### Add to `packages/cli/src/commands/login.ts`

```typescript
const POLL_INTERVAL = 2_000;  // Poll every 2 seconds
const MAX_POLL_ATTEMPTS = 60;  // 2 minutes max

interface DeviceCodeResponse {
  device_code: string;      // Internal code for polling
  user_code: string;        // Code user enters (e.g., "XYZW-1234")
  verification_url: string; // URL to visit
  expires_in: number;       // Seconds until expiry
  interval: number;         // Recommended poll interval
}

interface DeviceTokenResponse {
  access_token: string;
  refresh_token?: string;
  expires_at: string;
  email: string;
  tier: "free" | "premium";
  user_id: string;
}

interface DeviceTokenError {
  error: string;
  error_description?: string;
}

async function loginRemote(options: LoginOptions): Promise<void> {
  console.log(chalk.dim("Initiating device code authentication...\n"));
  
  // Request device code from API
  const deviceCodeResponse = await fetch(`${PREMIUM_URL}/api/cli/device-code`, {
    method: "POST",
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    body: JSON.stringify({ client_id: "jfp-cli" }),
  });
  
  if (!deviceCodeResponse.ok) {
    console.log(chalk.red("Failed to initiate authentication"));
    process.exit(1);
  }
  
  const { device_code, user_code, verification_url, interval } = 
    await deviceCodeResponse.json() as DeviceCodeResponse;
  
  // Display instructions to user
  console.log(boxen(
    `To sign in, visit:\n\n` +
    `  ${chalk.cyan(verification_url)}\n\n` +
    `And enter code:\n\n` +
    `  ${chalk.bold.yellow(formatUserCode(user_code))}`,
    { 
      padding: 1, 
      margin: 1, 
      borderStyle: "round",
      title: "Device Authentication",
      titleAlignment: "center",
    }
  ));
  
  console.log(chalk.dim("\nWaiting for authentication..."));
  console.log(chalk.dim("(Press Ctrl+C to cancel)\n"));
  
  // Poll for token
  const pollInterval = Math.max(interval * 1000, POLL_INTERVAL);
  let attempts = 0;
  
  while (attempts < MAX_POLL_ATTEMPTS) {
    await sleep(pollInterval);
    attempts++;
    
    try {
      const tokenResponse = await fetch(`${PREMIUM_URL}/api/cli/device-token`, {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({ 
          device_code,
          client_id: "jfp-cli",
        }),
      });
      
      if (tokenResponse.ok) {
        const credentials = await tokenResponse.json() as DeviceTokenResponse;
        
        // Save credentials
        await saveCredentials({
          access_token: credentials.access_token,
          refresh_token: credentials.refresh_token,
          expires_at: credentials.expires_at,
          email: credentials.email,
          tier: credentials.tier,
          user_id: credentials.user_id,
        });
        
        console.log(boxen(
          `${chalk.green("✓")} Logged in as ${chalk.bold(credentials.email)}\n` +
          `Tier: ${chalk.cyan(credentials.tier)}`,
          { padding: 1, margin: 1, borderStyle: "round", borderColor: "green" }
        ));
        
        return;
      }
      
      // Check for specific errors
      const errorBody = await tokenResponse.json() as DeviceTokenError;
      
      if (errorBody.error === "authorization_pending") {
        // User hasn't completed auth yet, keep polling
        process.stdout.write(".");
        continue;
      }
      
      if (errorBody.error === "slow_down") {
        // API wants us to slow down
        await sleep(5000);
        continue;
      }
      
      if (errorBody.error === "expired_token") {
        console.log(chalk.red("\n\nDevice code expired. Please try again."));
        process.exit(1);
      }
      
      if (errorBody.error === "access_denied") {
        console.log(chalk.red("\n\nAuthentication was denied."));
        process.exit(1);
      }
      
      // Unknown error
      console.log(chalk.red("\n\nAuthentication failed:"), errorBody.error_description || errorBody.error);
      process.exit(1);
      
    } catch (err) {
      // Network error, keep trying
      process.stdout.write("x");
    }
  }
  
  console.log(chalk.red("\n\nAuthentication timed out. Please try again."));
  process.exit(1);
}

function formatUserCode(code: string): string {
  // Format as "XXXX-XXXX" for easier reading
  if (code.length === 8 && !code.includes("-")) {
    return `${code.slice(0, 4)}-${code.slice(4)}`;
  }
  return code;
}

function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
  return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
}
```

## User Experience

### Terminal Output
```
$ jfp login --remote

╭─────────────────────────────────────╮
│      Device Authentication          │
│                                     │
│  To sign in, visit:                 │
│                                     │
│    https://premium.jeffreys...      │
│                                     │
│  And enter code:                    │
│                                     │
│    XYZW-1234                        │
│                                     │
╰─────────────────────────────────────╯

Waiting for authentication...
(Press Ctrl+C to cancel)

....

╭────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓ Logged in as user@example.com    │
│ Tier: premium                      │
╰────────────────────────────────────╯
```

## API Endpoints Required (Premium Site)
See separate tasks:
- `POST /api/cli/device-code` - Generate device code
- `POST /api/cli/device-token` - Exchange device code for token

## Error States
- `authorization_pending` - Keep polling (normal)
- `slow_down` - Wait longer between polls
- `expired_token` - Code expired, restart flow
- `access_denied` - User denied or cancelled

## Reference
- RFC 8628: OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant
- GitHub CLI: https://cli.github.com/manual/gh_auth_login

## Verification
- `jfp login --remote` shows code correctly
- User can complete flow on different device
- Polling detects completion
- Timeout handled gracefully","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:10:30.12594082-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:30:42.378172647-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T20:30:42.378172647-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented device code flow in loginRemote function with: device code request, user code formatting, polling for token exchange, error handling for expired_token/access_denied/network errors, and timeout handling. Added comprehensive tests.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-n299","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-tyb1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:13:26.194795261-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-n3p","title":"Create build-data.ts script for data pipeline","description":"**Details:**
- Main build script that generates all static data files
- Validates prompts against schema
- Generates registry.json and registry.manifest.json
- Computes checksums for cache validation
- Reports errors with clear messages

**Script Responsibilities:**
1. Import prompts from packages/core
2. Validate all prompts against Zod schema
3. Generate registry.json with full payload
4. Generate registry.manifest.json with checksums
5. Write files to apps/web/public/
6. Output summary statistics

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// scripts/build-data.ts
import { prompts, bundles, workflows } from '@jeffreysprompts/core/prompts';
import { promptSchema } from '@jeffreysprompts/core/prompts/schema';
import { createHash } from 'crypto';

async function main() {
  console.log('Building data assets...');
  
  // Validate prompts
  const errors: string[] = [];
  for (const prompt of prompts) {
    const result = promptSchema.safeParse(prompt);
    if (!result.success) {
      errors.push(`${prompt.id}: ${result.error.message}`);
    }
  }
  
  if (errors.length) {
    console.error('Validation errors:', errors);
    process.exit(1);
  }
  
  // Build registry payload
  const registry = {
    schemaVersion: 1,
    version: process.env.JFP_REGISTRY_VERSION ?? '0.0.0',
    updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    prompts,
    bundles,
    workflows,
    meta: {
      totalPromptCount: prompts.length,
      categories: [...new Set(prompts.map(p => p.category))],
      tags: [...new Set(prompts.flatMap(p => p.tags))].sort()
    }
  };
  
  // Compute checksums
  const registryJson = JSON.stringify(registry);
  const registryHash = createHash('sha256').update(registryJson).digest('hex');
  
  const manifest = {
    version: registry.version,
    generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    checksums: {
      registry: `sha256:${registryHash}`,
      prompts: Object.fromEntries(
        prompts.map(p => [p.id, `sha256:${createHash('sha256').update(p.content).digest('hex')}`])
      )
    }
  };
  
  // Write files
  await Bun.write('apps/web/public/registry.json', registryJson);
  await Bun.write('apps/web/public/registry.manifest.json', JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2));
  
  console.log(`✓ Generated registry.json (${prompts.length} prompts)`);
  console.log(`✓ Generated registry.manifest.json`);
}

main();
```

**Run in CI:**
- Part of build step before Next.js build
- Fails CI if validation errors

**Location:** scripts/build-data.ts

**Reasoning:** Centralized data pipeline ensures registry consistency. Checksum manifest enables efficient cache validation by CLI and PWA.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:35:24.147772264-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:28:41.560408998-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:28:41.560408998-05:00","close_reason":"Build scripts created: build-cli.sh, build-releases.sh, build-data.ts for registry generation","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-n3p","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-oc2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:57.240400934-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ogel","title":"Fix page.tsx: Replace zinc/indigo colors with neutral","description":"# Task: Unify colors in page.tsx

## Context
The main home page (page.tsx) was not updated during the initial UI overhaul, leaving it with the old color scheme while child components use the new palette.

## Specific Changes Required

### Background colors (zinc → neutral)
- Line 133: `bg-zinc-50 dark:bg-zinc-950` → `bg-neutral-50 dark:bg-neutral-950`
- Line 276: Same pattern in Suspense fallback

### Text colors (zinc → neutral)
- Line 188: `text-zinc-900 dark:text-white` → `text-neutral-900 dark:text-white`
- Line 197: `text-zinc-500 dark:text-zinc-400` → `text-neutral-500 dark:text-neutral-400`
- Line 219-232: Footer text colors

### Footer section (lines 215-261)
- Line 215: `dark:border-zinc-800 bg-zinc-50 dark:bg-zinc-900` → `dark:border-neutral-800 bg-neutral-50 dark:bg-neutral-900`
- Line 250: `border-zinc-200 dark:border-zinc-800` → `border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-800`

### Accent colors (indigo → neutral or primary)
- Line 168: "Clear all filters" button - `text-indigo-600 hover:text-indigo-800` → Consider `text-neutral-600 hover:text-neutral-900` or use primary CSS variable
- Line 242: "Try Pro" badge - KEEP indigo (intentional Pro branding)
- Line 256: Twitter link - KEEP indigo (external link accent)

## Design Rationale
- The main page background should match the Hero section (which uses neutral)
- Text colors should be consistent with cards and filters
- "Clear all filters" is a functional link, not a brand element, so it should use neutral
- Pro/external links can retain indigo as intentional accent

## Testing
1. Visual comparison in light mode
2. Visual comparison in dark mode
3. Verify all text remains readable (contrast check)
4. Verify no visual jarring between Hero → Filters → Grid

## Related Files
- This change prepares consistency for FeaturedPromptsSection (child)
- Should match Hero.tsx, CategoryFilter.tsx, TagFilter.tsx patterns","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:34:42.429379427-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T00:34:42.429379427-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ogel","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-s6ex","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:36:37.764755913-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Task
Create E2E tests for keyboard shortcuts functionality.

## Test Scenarios

### Global Shortcuts
1. Cmd/Ctrl+K opens command palette
2. Escape closes modals/overlays
3. / focuses search
4. ? shows shortcuts help
5. G then H goes home

### Navigation Shortcuts
6. G then L goes to library
7. G then S goes to search
8. G then P goes to profile
9. G then B goes to billing

### Content Shortcuts
10. N creates new prompt
11. E edits current prompt
12. C copies prompt content
13. S saves to library
14. D deletes (with confirm)

### Selection Shortcuts
15. J/K moves up/down in lists
16. X toggles selection
17. Shift+click multi-select
18. Cmd/Ctrl+A select all

### Shortcut Help
19. Help modal shows all shortcuts
20. Shortcuts grouped logically
21. Searchable shortcuts list

## Files to Create
- e2e/shortcuts/global.spec.ts
- e2e/shortcuts/navigation.spec.ts
- e2e/shortcuts/content.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/shortcuts-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria
- All shortcuts work reliably
- No conflicts with browser shortcuts
- Help accessible everywhere

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-s5as","title":"Implement mobile-first responsive design","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-shm. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Implement mobile-first responsive design for all pages.

## Why This Matters
From README: "We optimize for an elite UX across desktop and mobile."

## Responsive Breakpoints
Using Tailwind's default breakpoints:
- `sm`: 640px (large phones)
- `md`: 768px (tablets)
- `lg`: 1024px (laptops)
- `xl`: 1280px (desktops)
- `2xl`: 1536px (large desktops)

## Mobile-First Patterns

### 1. Navigation
- Hamburger menu on mobile
- Slide-out drawer for navigation
- Bottom tab bar for primary actions
- Collapsible header on scroll

### 2. Content Layout
- Single column on mobile
- Multi-column grid on desktop
- Cards stack vertically on mobile
- Touch-friendly spacing (min 44px targets)

### 3. Forms
- Full-width inputs on mobile
- Stack labels above inputs
- Large touch targets for buttons
- Keyboard-optimized input types

### 4. Tables
- Horizontal scroll with sticky column
- Or convert to card layout on mobile
- Collapsible rows for detail

### 5. Modals
- Full-screen on mobile
- Slide-up sheet pattern
- Easy dismiss with swipe
- Fixed footer for actions

## Component Updates

### Mobile Header
```typescript
// src/components/layout/mobile-header.tsx
export function MobileHeader() {
  return (
    <header className="lg:hidden fixed top-0 inset-x-0 h-14 ...">
      <HamburgerButton />
      <Logo />
      <UserMenu />
    </header>
  );
}
```

### Mobile Navigation
```typescript
// src/components/layout/mobile-nav.tsx
export function MobileNav({ isOpen, onClose }: Props) {
  return (
    <Sheet open={isOpen} onOpenChange={onClose}>
      <SheetContent side="left">
        <nav className="flex flex-col space-y-4">
          {/* Navigation items */}
        </nav>
      </SheetContent>
    </Sheet>
  );
}
```

### Responsive Grid
```typescript
<div className="grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4 gap-4">
  {items.map(item => <Card key={item.id} {...item} />)}
</div>
```

## Touch Interactions
- Swipe gestures for navigation
- Pull-to-refresh where appropriate
- Long-press for context menus
- Haptic feedback (where supported)

## Performance on Mobile
- Lazy load images below fold
- Reduce initial bundle for mobile
- Optimize for slower connections
- Respect data saver mode

## Testing Requirements
- Test on actual devices (iOS Safari, Android Chrome)
- Test in Playwright mobile viewport
- Test touch interactions
- Test with slow network throttling

## Files to Create
- `src/components/layout/mobile-header.tsx`
- `src/components/layout/mobile-nav.tsx`
- `src/components/layout/mobile-tab-bar.tsx`
- `src/components/ui/swipe-sheet.tsx`
- `src/hooks/use-mobile.ts`

## Success Criteria
- All pages usable on mobile
- Touch targets are adequate size
- Navigation is intuitive
- Performance is acceptable on 3G
- No horizontal overflow

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-s6ex","title":"UI Color Consistency: Unify zinc/indigo to neutral palette","description":"# Epic: UI Color Consistency Overhaul

## Background & Problem Statement

During the Stripe/Linear-inspired UI overhaul, we refactored core components (PromptCard, Hero, CategoryFilter, TagFilter, PromptGrid) to use a consistent `neutral-*` color palette. However, a comprehensive audit revealed that many other files still use the old `zinc-*` and `indigo-*` color schemes, creating visual inconsistency across the application.

### Current State (Inconsistent)
- **Refactored components**: Use `neutral-*` (correct)
- **page.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` (14 instances) + `indigo-*` (6 instances)
- **FeaturedPromptsSection.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` + `indigo-*`
- **PromptDetailModal.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` (20+ instances) + `indigo-*` (5+ instances)
- **SpotlightSearch.tsx**: Uses `indigo-600` for selected states
- **SwipeablePromptCard.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` throughout
- **BasketSidebar.tsx**: May have inconsistencies

### Why This Matters
1. **Visual cohesion**: Users perceive inconsistent colors as "unpolished"
2. **Dark mode**: `zinc` and `neutral` have slightly different hue values in OKLCH
3. **Maintainability**: Multiple color palettes = confusion for future changes
4. **Design system integrity**: We defined `neutral-*` as the standard

### Design Decision
- **Primary text/bg**: `neutral-*` scale (consistent with Tailwind defaults)
- **Accent colors**: Use CSS variables (`primary`, `muted`, etc.) when possible
- **When `indigo-*` is needed**: Only for special emphasis (Pro badges, semantic search indicator)

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Zero instances of `zinc-*` in component files (except intentional use)
- [ ] `indigo-*` only used for specific Pro/premium features
- [ ] All interactive states use `neutral-*` palette
- [ ] Dark mode contrast maintained (WCAG AA)
- [ ] Build passes with no visual regressions

### Files to Modify
1. apps/web/src/app/page.tsx
2. apps/web/src/components/landing/FeaturedPromptsSection.tsx
3. apps/web/src/components/PromptDetailModal.tsx
4. apps/web/src/components/SpotlightSearch.tsx
5. apps/web/src/components/mobile/SwipeablePromptCard.tsx
6. apps/web/src/components/BasketSidebar.tsx (if needed)

### Estimated Scope
- 6 files to modify
- ~50-80 color class replacements
- Low risk (visual only, no logic changes)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:34:19.74681817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T00:34:19.74681817-05:00"}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-skwl","title":"Public site: add Pro CTA + Login in Nav","description":"## Goal
Add a clear, tasteful path to Pro from the public site without undermining the free-first UX. Users should easily find:
- **Go Pro** (primary CTA)
- **Login** (secondary link for existing Pro users)

## Primary Placement
- **Component:** `apps/web/src/components/Nav.tsx`
- **Entry point:** `apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx` renders `<Nav />`

## UX & Visual Requirements
- **Free-first:** CTA should be visible but not dominant.
- **Desktop:**
  - Secondary text link: **Login**
  - Primary button-like link: **Go Pro** (or **Upgrade** if copy fits better)
- **Mobile:**
  - Add both links to the mobile sheet/menu.
  - **Go Pro** must be visually stronger than **Login**.
- **Don’t break layout:** no wrapping or overflow at common widths.

## URL & Routing
- Base URL: `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`
- CTA: `${PRO_URL}/` (Go Pro)
- Login: `${PRO_URL}/login`

## Implementation Details
1) **Add a public env var**
   - `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRO_URL` with default fallback to `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`.
   - Update `apps/web/src/lib/env.ts` (or relevant env schema) to include it.

2) **Centralize URL usage**
   - In `Nav.tsx`, define:
     ```ts
     const PRO_URL = env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PRO_URL ?? "https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com";
     const proHref = `${PRO_URL}/`;
     const loginHref = `${PRO_URL}/login`;
     ```

3) **Styling**
   - Use existing button/link styles in the nav to match the design system.
   - Prefer shadcn `Button` or existing Tailwind utility classes used in `Nav.tsx`.

## Analytics (Optional but Preferred)
If analytics helper exists (e.g., `apps/web/src/lib/analytics.ts`), add events:
- `pro_cta_clicked` (button)
- `pro_login_clicked` (link)
Attach context: `{ placement: "nav", device: "desktop|mobile" }` if available.

## Edge Cases
- If `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRO_URL` is missing, default to hardcoded `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`.
- Ensure external link safety if using `target="_blank"`: include `rel="noopener noreferrer"`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Desktop and mobile nav both show **Go Pro** and **Login**.
- Both links route to Pro site endpoints above.
- Styles match the current nav and preserve free-first tone.
- No layout regression on small screens.
- (Optional) Analytics events fire on click.
